diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Digested (coffee-ground) blood in vomit localises bleeding to a source proximal to the ligament of Treitz.
Haematemesis is not a feature of an isolated lower GI source.
Melaena reflects digested blood from an upper GI source; with haematemesis it confirms significant upper GI bleeding.
The foul-smelling, tarry quality plus concurrent haematemesis and shock argue against benign discolouration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural pre-syncope / near-fainting on sitting up

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural symptoms indicate a significant intravascular volume deficit from blood loss.
Compensatory tachycardia is an early, sensitive sign of haemorrhagic volume loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Borderline-low supine blood pressure (95/60) reported by carer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A borderline-low supine BP in a compensating older adult signals major blood loss; carer-reported and unverified but decision-relevant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor, diaphoresis, cold peripheries and drowsiness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral vasoconstriction and early cerebral hypoperfusion indicate decompensating shock.
Non-selective NSAIDs are a leading cause of peptic ulceration and upper GI bleeding, especially in frail older adults.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed capillary refill (4 s) and weak thready pulse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective peripheral hypoperfusion — a clinician-measured sign, sealed; the AI Doctor infers shock from the carer's pallor/cold/faint reports, not this measurement.
Direct confirmation of melaena; a clinician examination not available via telehealth — sealed answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Actively bleeding gastric/duodenal ulcer on upper endoscopy

investigation_details.expected_result: Actively bleeding gastric or duodenal peptic ulcer amenable to endoscopic haemostasis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed opportunity for definitive haemostasis; continued bleeding and death.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Endoscopy is the gold-standard confirmation and treatment; hospital-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe fall in serum haemoglobin

investigation_details.expected_result: Acute significant drop in haemoglobin; group and cross-match for transfusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies blood loss; guides transfusion — laboratory investigation, sealed.
Absence of fever and watery diarrhoea argues against an infective gastroenteritis as the cause of the black stool.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Massive upper GI bleed with haemorrhagic shock
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage with hypovolaemic shock
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Haematemesis + melaena + tachycardia + postural pre-syncope + peripheral shutdown together define a life-threatening bleed requiring immediate emergency transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate emergency ambulance transfer for resuscitation, transfusion and urgent endoscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive assessment and treatment are hospital-only; the AI Doctor's role is to trigger emergency transfer, not to arrange outpatient tests.
Identifies and treats the bleeding ulcer; performed after resuscitation in hospital.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, group and cross-match, coagulation, U&E (in hospital)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies blood loss, prepares transfusion and assesses renal function/urea (raised in upper GI bleed).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Postural blood-pressure (tilt) testing at the facility
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Contraindicated here — deliberately standing a shocked patient risks syncope and injury; postural compromise is already clinically evident.
